lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hi, I need fuel for a lamp.

lines.speaker: Chemist
lines.text: Hello there, the fuel you need is lamp oil, do you need help making it?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes please.

lines.speaker: Chemist
lines.text: It's really quite simple. You use the small still in here. It's all set up, so there's no fiddling around with dials...

lines.speaker: Chemist
lines.text: Just put ordinary swamp tar in, and then use a lantern or lamp to get the oil out.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Thanks.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No thanks.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello.

lines.speaker: Chemist
lines.text: Oh.. hello, how's it going?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Good thanks.

lines.speaker: Chemist
lines.text: Good to hear, sorry but I have a few things to do right now.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well I'd better let you get on then.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I have a slightly odd question.

lines.speaker: Chemist
lines.text: Jolly good, the odder the better. I like oddities.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Do you know how I might distil a mix of anchovy oil and flowers so that it forms a layer on the inside of a butterfly jar?

lines.speaker: Chemist
lines.text: That is an odd question. I commend you for it. Why would you want to do that?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Apparently, if I can make a jar like this it will be useful for storing implings in.

lines.speaker: Chemist
lines.text: So, do you have any of this fish-and-flower flavoured oil and a butterfly jar then?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: As it happens, I do.

lines.speaker: Chemist
lines.text: Well then. Hmmm. My lamp oil still may be able to do what you want. Give it a go and see if it works.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Thanks!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Actually, no.

lines.speaker: Chemist
lines.text: If you go and get them then I may be able to help you. I'm better at coming up with answers if the questions are in my hands.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So, how do you make anchovy oil?

lines.speaker: Chemist
lines.text: Anchovies are pretty oily fish. I'd have thought you could just grind them up and sieve out the bits. You'd probably want to remove any water first - Cooking should do that pretty well.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Do you have a sieve I can use?

lines.speaker: Chemist
lines.text: Errm, yes. But you already have one. Two sieves is a bit excessive, don't you think?

lines.speaker: Chemist
lines.text: Are you sure you don't already have one?

lines.speaker: Chemist
lines.text: Errm, yes. Here, have this one. It's only been used for sieving dead rats out of sewer water.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Err, why? Actually, on second thoughts I don't want to know.

lines.speaker: Chemist
lines.text: Well, it should be ideally suited to your task.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Fair enough.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'd better go and get the repellent.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I think so.

lines.speaker: Chemist
lines.text: Well done, have this.
